subject: MATHEMATICS
title: Equal Groups and Facts
objective: Model multiplication with equal groups and arrays.
introduction: Multiplication represents equal groups; 3 groups of 4 has 12 objects. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Equal Groups and Facts”.
A key word used in the lesson “Equal Groups and Facts”.
A key word used in the lesson “Equal Groups and Facts”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Multiplication represents equal groups; 3 groups of 4 has 12 objects.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Multiplication represents equal groups; 3 groups of 4 has 12 objects.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. 7 and 16 do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Equal Groups and Facts”?

options.text: Multiplication represents equal groups; 3 groups of 4 has 12 objects.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Multiplication represents equal groups; 3 groups of 4 has 12 objects.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Multiplication represents equal groups; 3 groups of 4 has 12 objects.
The correct response is “3×4=12” because it follows the lesson explanation: Multiplication represents equal groups; 3 groups of 4 has 12 objects.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Unequal groups without adjustment” because it follows the lesson explanation: Multiplication represents equal groups; 3 groups of 4 has 12 objects.
The correct response is “12” because it follows the lesson explanation: Multiplication represents equal groups; 3 groups of 4 has 12 objects.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “5 groups of 3=15” because it follows the lesson explanation: Multiplication represents equal groups; 3 groups of 4 has 12 objects.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Equal Groups and Facts; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Equal Groups and Facts.
